contest.title: 2025-2026 ICPC, NERC, Northern Eurasia Finals (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Official NERC tutorial; used to confirm the transformation a_i=b_i+b_{i-1}, the odd/even cycle cases, and the max/max-sum lower-bound formula.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: Recover the minimum possible number of rounds in a circular comparison game from the counts a_i of wins adjacent to each participant.
annotation.constraints: The circle can be large, so the hidden edge-round counts b_i must be recovered in linear time.
annotation.core_idea: Let b_i be the number of rounds where participant i's number is strictly larger than the next participant's number. Then each reported a_i equals b_i + b_{i-1}. If all b_i were known, the minimum rounds is max(max b_i, ceil(sum b_i/(n-1))) because each round can increase any subset of b_i except all n of them. For odd n, the cyclic linear system has a unique solution. For even n, all solutions are b_i = base_i + (-1)^i Delta; choose Delta so all b_i are nonnegative and max b_i is minimized, then apply the same lower-bound formula.

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: Solve a_i=b_i+b_{i-1} on the cycle, optimize the even-n free parameter, then compute the tight lower bound for the number of rounds.

tags.evidence: The central transformation is solving the cyclic linear system a_i=b_i+b_{i-1}.
tags.description: Solve or characterize linear equations over numbers, including structured systems with free parameters.
tags.created_reason: This is distinct from matrix exponentiation or xor basis and useful for algebraic reconstruction problems.

tags.evidence: The final answer is the maximum of two tight lower bounds: max b_i and ceil(sum b_i/(n-1)).
The solution splits on odd versus even n because the cyclic system has different degrees of freedom.
For even n, all feasible solutions are parameterized by Delta, chosen to minimize max(r0+Delta, r1-Delta) under nonnegativity.
tags.description: Reduce a family of feasible solutions to one scalar parameter and optimize it analytically.
tags.created_reason: This appears in structured equation systems and balancing problems, and is more precise than generic binary search.
